dimensions.note: No secrets handled or stored. Credentials come from the ambient AWS chain (profiles empty) or named profiles via fromIni({ profile }); EC2Client/STSClient are constructed with that provider, nothing is vaulted. requiredProfileSuffix is a non-secret guard, regions are non-secret config. No credential value is ever logged — warn lines log the profile label or <ambient>, never the provider.

Structured placeholders throughout: info on per-account start (account/profile/region count) and on completion (vpc + error totals); warn on a refused-suffix profile, a credential failure, a no-VpcId VPC, and a per-region describe failure. All use the {placeholder} + props form (e.g. logger.info("Inventorying account {account} ...", { account, profile, count })) — no string interpolation, no secrets in messages or props.

All throws/validation precede writeResource: GlobalArgsSchema.parse and resolveBootstrapRegion run before any AWS call. Per-target and per-region failures are caught and converted to scan_error rows via writeError rather than aborting the sweep, with classifyError distinguishing auth_expired (transient, operator runs aws sso login), access_denied (permanent SCP/IAM), and other. classifyError checks access-denial signals before token-expiry to avoid an SSO-role ARN in an AccessDenied message misfiring as auth_expired. try/catch is scoped narrowly to each getAccountId / describeEnabledRegions / describeVpcs call.

tests/aws_vpc_inventory_test.ts covers pure helpers (tagsFromAws, accountNameFromProfile, collectCidrBlocks state-filter+dedupe, classifyError including the SSO-role-ARN regression, resolveBootstrapRegion, vpcKey/scanErrorKey) and runScan via createModelTestContext + an injected AwsApi replay: happy path (account-name derivation, shared-in flagging, multi-CIDR), plus failure paths (suffix refusal, credential expiry skips one account but not the fleet, per-region access_denied still scans siblings, describe_regions failure). tests/aws_vpc_inventory_smoke_test.ts replays a two-account x two-region fan-out end-to-end through an in-memory facade, asserts the refused profile never touches the API, and that dataHandles == vpcCount + errorCount. The colocated aws_vpc_inventory_test.ts pins manifest/version sync and the upgrade-chain-ends-at-version invariant. No live SDK or network is touched.

Read-only sweep with deterministic spec-prefixed instance keys: vpcKey = vpc-<accountId>-<region>-<vpcId> and scanErrorKey = error-<profileLabel|ambient>-<region|account>-<phase>, so re-runs overwrite the same instances rather than duplicating. No cloud resources are created, so partial interruption leaves nothing to orphan — at worst a subset of vpc/scan_error rows are written and a re-run reconciles them. The smoke suite asserts one expired account does not abort the rest of the fleet.

describeVpcs paginates DescribeVpcsCommand to exhaustion via NextToken (MaxResults 100); getAccountId and describeEnabledRegions are single non-paginated calls. AWS response shapes are treated as all-optional and defaulted before use (resp.Account ?? "", resp.Regions ?? [] with a typeof-string filter, resp.Vpcs ?? [], v.VpcId ?? "", tagsFromAws/collectCidrBlocks tolerate missing arrays/fields). All clients are constructed with maxAttempts: 5 so the SDK retries throttling/5xx with its default backoff.

EC2Client is created per (region) and STSClient per account inside the SDK facade for read-only Describe/GetCallerIdentity calls; the AWS SDK v3 HTTP handler manages its own connection pool and needs no explicit teardown. No file handles, sockets, or temp files are opened, and no cloud resources are created, so there is nothing to track for cleanup.

Re-reviewed after scrubbing the classifyError SSO-ARN regression fixture in tests/aws_vpc_inventory_test.ts, which previously embedded a real 12-digit account id (688375758630), a real company name in the SSO permission-set (AWSReservedSSO_SitooReadOnlyAccess_xyz), and a real-ish email (m@s.com). It is now a fully synthetic ARN (arn:aws:sts::111111111111:assumed-role/AWSReservedSSO_ReadOnlyAccess_abc123/alice@example.com) that preserves the AWSReservedSSO_ substring and the 'not authorized' AccessDenied wording so the regression keeps its teeth (the test still asserts access_denied, not auth_expired). grep -rniE 'sitoo|vanta|aikido|688375758630|m@s\.com' over the whole extension dir now returns zero hits. README.md contains zero 12-digit account-id literals and zero IPv4 address literals (grep-verified); its CEL/CLI examples use placeholder profile names (prod-platform-readonly) and a generic data.latest(...) template. Test fixtures use synthetic repeated-digit account ids (111111111111, 999999999999, etc.), RFC1918 CIDRs, and synthetic vpc-/profile identifiers. Zero real infrastructure or vendor identifiers remain on the published surface.

VpcRecordSchema and ScanErrorSchema are plain z.object (no .passthrough()); tags use z.record(string,string), kind is a z.enum, scannedAt is z.iso.datetime(). Public resource shapes are declared as explicit exported interfaces (VpcRecord, ScanError) rather than z.infer aliases, keeping deno doc --lint clean and the CEL-queryable surface fixed.
Both the vpc and scan_error specs declare lifetime: 'infinite' and garbageCollection: 10.

Read-only inventory/discovery model, not a CRUD lifecycle. The single scan method is a factory that re-derives all vpc + scan_error rows each run via Describe*/GetCallerIdentity; there is no AWS resource this model owns to create/update/delete. Re-running scan is itself the drift-detection mechanism, so a separate sync method is unnecessary.

scan performs only read-only sts:GetCallerIdentity, ec2:DescribeRegions, and ec2:DescribeVpcs calls; there is no mutating action a policy/live preflight label would gate. The requiredProfileSuffix guard already fails a non-read-only profile closed before any AWS call.

Mechanical check: vpc keys are 'vpc-<accountId>-<region>-<vpcId>' and scan_error keys are 'error-<profileLabel>-<region>-<phase>', so the two specs cannot collide even when a VPC id and a profile share a bare token. Each spec is written by exactly one code path (vpc by the per-VPC branch, scan_error by writeError) with a consistent schema shape, and the account/region components keep keys unique across the fan-out.

Single-pass factory writer: runScan only calls context.writeResource and never reads back previously stored swamp state (no update/delete/sync method, no readResource or dataRepository access), so the readResource-vs-dataRepository guidance does not apply.

Initial publish. model.upgrades is a single no-op entry whose toVersion (2026.06.13.0) equals model.version, establishing the migration chain so any future schema-changing upgrade chains cleanly from here; upgradeAttributes is the identity (old) => old, correct because there is no prior stored shape to migrate. The colocated test asserts upgrades.at(-1).toVersion === model.version so an SDK-bump batch cannot silently drift the chain.
